metrics.narrative: The business problem is well articulated qualitatively but no dollar-quantified ROI or savings figure appears anywhere in the calls read. On 2026-01-14, the initiative was framed as replacing 'ongoing indecision and conflict regarding responses and actions, leading to a lack of conclusive decisions and ineffective communication with bidders,' and the scope was described as shifting 'from full Source-to-Pay to a Supply Information Management platform encompassing eProcurement, spend analysis, collaboration, performance and development.' On 2026-03-10 a named pain point was 'an urgent need for an iFAX module and a supply information module, along with significant improvements in performance and dashboards, which have become increasingly difficult to manage.' None of this has been translated into a quantified business case for the economic buyer.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 (Zycus - S&C Updated Response Discussion)
champion.narrative: Dan Chao and Katie Booth (Global Indirect Sourcing Manager - IT) function as the deal's working champions: Dan Chao has driven scheduling, requirements reconciliation and scope negotiation on nearly every call from January through 2026-06-30, and pushed back constructively on Zycus's direct-materials gaps rather than disqualifying the deal outright. Katie Booth gave Zycus practical demo-planning advice ('if you think you need an hour, consider an hour and a half,' 2026-06-30) and voiced explicit support ('you guys are still in a very good shape to what I can see now,' 2026-06-24). Neither is confirmed to have direct sponsorship access to Jim Boss or Kara Weiner in the read evidence, so this is a developing champion pair rather than a confirmed champion with proven access to power.

competition.narrative: Oracle (the incumbent ERP, referenced specifically as 'the EBS version' and via Oracle MRP/UBS for direct materials) is the clearest and most current threat, reinforced by the Next Step note (2026-07-09) that S&C's VP Supply Chain is biased against Oracle and iValua because 'iValua and Oracle will ONLY do 3rd party' implementation, a stated Zycus differentiator. Coupa appears in the Salesforce competitor field and in living memory as a prior-noted rival but has no fresh evidence of active bidding in this window. A 2026-06-03 call also surfaced a real risk that Zycus itself might withdraw over direct-materials gaps versus Oracle's native capability, later mitigated by S&C narrowing the eProcurement scope to indirect-only.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well documented and evolves across the deal: early scoping (2026-01-14) named 'ongoing indecision and conflict regarding responses and actions' with bidders; by 2026-03-10 the need was 'an urgent need for an iFAX module and a supply information module, along with significant improvements in performance and dashboards.' A sharper, direct-materials-specific pain surfaced on 2026-06-03: Zycus itself flagged that it does not do well with direct/PLM/inventory requirements ('We just don't really do exceptionally well... the requirements changed. Maybe we're just not a good fit'), which S&C resolved by narrowing eProcurement scope to indirect-only, with direct materials marked TBD.

economic_buyer.narrative: Salesforce Contact Roles name Jim Boss (VP Global Operational Excellence) as both Decision Maker and Executive Sponsor, and Doug Bartow / Janki Parekh as Decision Makers, so the role is formally mapped. Call evidence also repeatedly names Kara Weiner (VP Supply Chain) as the executive driving the initiative and biased toward Zycus's in-house delivery model over third-party-dependent rivals (Next Step, 2026-07-09: 'VP SC Kara Weiner is biased toward in-house implementation and doesn't like 3rd party partners - iValua and Oracle will ONLY do 3rd party'). However, none of the calls read in this window (Jan-Jul 2026) show Jim Boss, Kara Weiner, Doug Bartow, or David Single directly on a call; all recent engagement (May-Jul 2026) has been with Dan Chao, Katie Booth, and Yufeng Chen. Access to power is named but not recently, directly engaged.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 (Zycus - S&C Updated Response Discussion)
decision_process.narrative: The process is explicit: RFP response deadline 2026-06-22, demo evaluation window 2026-06-22 to 2026-06-30 (2026-06-08 call), final technical RFP response and priced proposal submitted 2026-07-09 (today, per Next_Step__c), module-by-module demos with dedicated SMEs the week of 2026-07-13 to 2026-07-17, and a stated decision/award target of end of July 2026 (matching the 2026-07-31 close date). Dan Chao explicitly confirmed available demo windows on the 2026-06-30 call ('Wednesday, the 8th, or Friday, the 10th, could be opportunities to start on a few sections of the project... the week of the 13th through the 17th is fairly open').

decision_criteria.narrative: S&C built a detailed, numbered RFP (RFX01, RFX02, etc.) with 217 requirement lines across 9 modules, classified as out-of-box, configurable, custom, or custom-build, and a 'common scorecard approach' used by roughly 35 named SME evaluators (2026-05-14 call: 'A common scorecard approach will be used for evaluation, focusing on both detailed module functionality and strategic supplier capability including future roadmap enhancements'; 2026-01-30 call named a 'multi-dimensional evaluation matrix covering UX, change management, AI strategy, supplier support, and total cost'). This is one of the most explicit, well-documented decision-criteria pictures in the deal.

north_star_verdict.math: 16 days to close (today 2026-07-08 -> 2026-07-24). Deal has been in an RFP/demo cycle since a revised 217-requirement SOW went out 14-16 May 2026; final RFP submission was reset on 18 Jun 2026 to 18 Jul 2026, leaving only 6 days between requirements-lock and the recorded close date for demo scoring, executive review, pricing, and contracting -- none of which (beyond pricing) has visibly started. north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer named Zycus Vendor of Choice on 24 Jun 2026, but the 24 Jul close date is not credible: final RFP submission isn't due until 18 Jul, no MSA/SOW/legal work has started, and Corporate Counsel has never appeared on a call.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Push to Best Case with a realistic close in September 2026, allowing time after the 18 Jul RFP submission for demo scoring, executive review, pricing sign-off, and a paper process that has not yet begun.

flags: No quantified business case: qualitative pain (vendor-led implementation need, change-management constraint) is documented but no ROI, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact. Economic Buyer and Executive Sponsor have not articulated financial metrics. This weakens forecast defensibility and leaves the deal vulnerable if budget or priority shifts.

Paper-process timeline gap: 18 days to close, no documented MSA/SOW milestones, legal-review process, or InfoSec engagement despite Harman Toor (Corporate Counsel) in contact roles. Risk of contracting delay if not surfaced and planned in final demo conversation.

Economic buyer passive: Jim Boss (Executive Sponsor, four contact roles) and David Single (VP Finance, three contact roles) are mapped but have not engaged on pricing, ROI, or budget sign-off across 14 calls. The move: escalate to the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) for an executive connect to the deal owner's manager or David Single to secure budget approval and ROI sign-off before contracting. Without EB active sponsorship, forecast stays Best Case and contracting timeline is at risk.

The direct-materials gap (PLM/3DX, VMI, OTM) is now openly disclosed in Zycus's own RFP response; use the 3 requested references (smooth/some-issues/problematic) to show how other manufacturing customers absorbed similar gaps, turning candor into a differentiator against Oracle and iValua.

Champion Katie Booth and coach Yufeng Chen are actively coaching Zycus on scoring mechanics and demo structure; convert that goodwill into a direct introduction to the relevant stakeholder Boss ahead of the early-July on-site executive review of demo outcomes.

Convert the verbal 'vendor of choice' signal into a written scoring understanding before demos start, protecting Zycus from being penalized on the 15-20% direct-materials deviation in the 217-line RFP.
The direct-materials gap is the only real technical objection; S&C's own champions said it won't sink the deal, so keep the app-based 3DX roadmap concrete and dated.

Lock in writing that direct materials won't be scored against Zycus for eProcurement, now that Yufeng has verbally confirmed it twice (24 and 30 Jun 2026), before the 35-evaluator scorecard is finalized.

Land the PLM/3DX wireframes and phased-development story convincingly at the final demo -- 15-20% of the 217 lines deviate, concentrated in eProcurement/direct materials, and the buyer has signaled this won't hurt scoring only if shown credibly.

Secure direct executive engagement (the named Decision Maker / Executive Sponsor and the VP Supply Chain) on the priced proposal submitted today, since all recent contact has stayed at the project-coordinator level (Dan Chao, Katie Booth, Yufeng Chen).
